blocks.content: Kit got the window open one inch at a time, teeth clenched against every sound. The latch was corroded but it turned.
Eli went first — swung out, hung from the sill, dropped. A soft thud in the grass. Savannah next, quieter, feet under her in a second.
The footsteps were on the upper landing now.
She went to the window. He helped her over the sill, her hands last on the frame, and for a moment they were eye to eye through it: her on the outside hanging on, him on the inside letting go.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Kit. Promise me you make it out.
She dropped. He heard her land. He spun back toward the window—
